q: Can foreigners buy property in the Bahamas?
a: Yes. The Bahamas warmly welcomes foreign buyers. There are no restrictions on non-nationals purchasing property here.

q: What is the buying process like?
a: We start with a free consultation to understand your needs, shortlist suitable properties, arrange viewings, guide you through the offer and legal process, and support you through to closing.

q: What are the upfront costs of buying?
a: Stamp duty is typically 2.5–10% of the purchase price, split between buyer and seller. Legal fees, inspection, and survey costs also apply. We provide a full cost breakdown upfront.

q: How long does the buying process take?
a: Typically 60–90 days from offer to closing, depending on financing and the legal process.

q: Do you handle rentals as well as sales?
a: Yes — we assist with long-term rental properties and investment rental listings. We also offer property management services for landlords.

q: Can I view properties remotely before visiting?
a: Yes. We offer virtual tours and detailed video walkthroughs for international clients before they commit to visiting.

q: What areas do you cover?
a: Nassau, Paradise Island, and connections across the family islands including Exuma, Eleuthera, and Abaco.

q: What types of properties do you have?
a: Homes, condos, beachfront villas, vacant land, commercial real estate, and investment properties across all price points.

q: Do I need a local lawyer to buy property?
a: Yes — a Bahamian attorney is required to handle the conveyancing. We have a network of trusted legal professionals we can refer you to.

q: What is property tax like in the Bahamas?
a: Annual property tax is due by March 31st each year. Rates vary by property value and whether it is owner-occupied. We can provide guidance specific to any property you're considering.

q: Do you offer property management?
a: Yes — we manage rental properties on behalf of owners including tenant sourcing, lease management, and maintenance coordination.

q: What is the minimum price for property in Nassau?
a: Entry-level condos and homes can start from the low $200,000s. Beachfront and luxury properties range significantly higher. We work across all budgets.

q: Is it a good time to buy in the Bahamas?
a: The Bahamas remains a strong market for both lifestyle buyers and investors. Our agents can share current market insights and help you make an informed decision.

q: Do you work with first-time buyers?
a: Absolutely. Many of our clients are first-time buyers — we guide you through every step including financing options, legal process, and what to expect.

q: Can I get a mortgage in the Bahamas as a foreigner?
a: Some local banks offer financing to qualified foreign buyers, though terms vary. Many international buyers also use financing from their home country.

q: What neighbourhoods are best in Nassau?
a: Popular areas include Lyford Cay, Old Fort Bay, Cable Beach, Paradise Island, and Port New Providence. The best fit depends on your lifestyle and budget — let's discuss.

q: Are there beachfront properties available?
a: Yes — beachfront and waterfront properties are among our most sought-after listings. Supply is limited so we recommend acting quickly when the right property appears.

q: Do you have luxury properties?
a: Yes — we handle luxury residential properties, estates, and high-end condos across Nassau, Paradise Island, and beyond.

q: Can I rent before I buy?
a: Absolutely. Many clients rent first to experience different neighbourhoods before committing to a purchase. We can help with both.

q: What is Stamp Duty?
a: Stamp Duty is the main property transfer tax in the Bahamas. It's calculated as a percentage of the purchase price and is typically shared between buyer and seller. We'll explain the exact figure for any property you're considering.

q: Do you handle commercial property?
a: Yes — we assist with commercial real estate including office space, retail, and investment properties.

q: How do I list my property for sale with you?
a: Get in touch via WhatsApp and one of our experienced agents will contact you to discuss your property, pricing strategy, and listing process.

q: How long does it typically take to sell a property?
a: It varies by property type and pricing. Well-priced properties in desirable areas can sell in weeks. Our agents will advise on realistic timelines.

q: Do you work with investors?
a: Yes — we have significant experience working with local and international investors seeking rental income properties and capital appreciation opportunities.

q: Can I buy land and build?
a: Yes — vacant land is available. We can help you find suitable parcels and connect you with trusted local architects and contractors.

q: Are there any ownership restrictions for foreigners?
a: No ownership restrictions for foreigners. However, certain large developments may have additional requirements — we'll walk you through anything relevant.

q: How do I get pre-qualified for financing?
a: If you need local financing we can refer you to Bahamian banks and mortgage brokers who specialise in working with buyers. Contact us to get started.

q: What is a typical agent commission?
a: Standard real estate commissions in the Bahamas are typically around 6% of the sale price. We are transparent about all fees upfront.

q: Is property ownership in the Bahamas safe?
a: Yes — the Bahamas has a well-established legal system for property ownership with clear title processes and strong protections for buyers.

q: Do you have properties outside Nassau?
a: Yes — we have connections and listings across the family islands including Exuma, Eleuthera, Abaco, and more.

q: Can you help with relocation to the Bahamas?
a: Absolutely. Many of our clients are relocating and we go beyond the property transaction to help you settle in — school connections, community introductions, and more.

q: What documents do I need to buy property?
a: Valid government ID, proof of funds or financing, and a Bahamian attorney. We'll provide a full checklist once you identify a property of interest.

q: What is the difference between freehold and leasehold?
a: Most property in the Bahamas is sold freehold (full ownership). Some developments are leasehold. We'll always clarify the ownership structure for any listing.

q: Can I visit Nassau and view properties in one trip?
a: Yes — we frequently arrange structured property tours for visiting clients. Let us know your travel dates and we'll plan a productive viewing schedule.

q: Are there new developments available?
a: Yes — we have access to new residential and mixed-use developments. These can offer good entry pricing and strong appreciation potential.

q: How do I get started?
a: Just message us here on WhatsApp with what you're looking for — budget, area, property type — and we'll set up a free consultation with one of our experienced agents.

q: Why choose Bahamas Realty?
a: Decades of local market knowledge, a trusted team of dedicated agents, a strong network of legal and financial professionals, and a track record of satisfied buyers and sellers across all price points.

q: Do you list properties on international portals?
a: Yes — our listings receive international exposure through our marketing network helping connect your property with qualified buyers worldwide.

q: What if I have more questions?
a: We're always happy to help. Just message us here on WhatsApp — our team responds fast and we love hearing from potential buyers, sellers, and investors.
